findings.title: Dangerous import: MmGetSystemRoutineAddress
findings.description: Resolves kernel export by name — used in DSE bypass to find CI!g_CiOptions

findings.title: Dangerous import: ExAllocatePoolWithTag
findings.description: Pool allocation — check for size validation

findings.title: WDF (Windows Driver Framework) driver detected
findings.description: Driver uses WDF framework (detected imports: WdfVersionBind). IOCTL dispatch uses EvtIoDeviceControl callback via WdfIoQueueCreate rather than DriverObject->MajorFunction[].

findings.title: IOCTL code: 0x00322400
findings.description: Method: METHOD_BUFFERED, Access: FILE_ANY_ACCESS

findings.title: IOCTL code: 0x00322404
findings.description: Method: METHOD_BUFFERED, Access: FILE_ANY_ACCESS

findings.title: IOCTL code: 0x00322408
findings.description: Method: METHOD_BUFFERED, Access: FILE_ANY_ACCESS

findings.title: IOCTL code: 0x0032240C
findings.description: Method: METHOD_BUFFERED, Access: FILE_ANY_ACCESS

findings.title: Hardcoded address: 0x2080000
findings.description: Hardcoded page-aligned address found. May be a physical memory address used with MmMapIoSpace or similar.

findings.title: Missing mitigation: Signature enforcement (FORCE_INTEGRITY)
findings.description: Signature enforcement (FORCE_INTEGRITY) is not enabled. Driver can load without a valid Authenticode signature.

findings.title: Driver is NOT HVCI-compatible
findings.description: Driver fails HVCI checks (missing FORCE_INTEGRITY or has W+X sections). Cannot load in HVCI-enabled environments without policy bypass.

findings.title: Kernel write gadgets found (8 instances)
findings.description: Multiple 'mov [reg+offset], reg' patterns in driver code. If the register is derived from user input (e.g., IOCTL buffer), this is an arbitrary kernel memory write primitive.

details.gadgets: 0x140001003: mov qword ptr [rax + 8], rbx; 0x140001007: mov qword ptr [rax + 0x10], rbp; 0x14000100B: mov qword ptr [rax + 0x18], rsi; 0x14000100F: mov qword ptr [rax + 0x20], rdi

findings.title: Hidden function with purpose 'runtime resolve' at 0x140007501
findings.description: Function at 0x140007501 performs 'runtime resolve' but is not reachable via the normal DriverEntry → MajorFunction call graph.  It may be invoked via a timer, DPC, work item, or kernel callback — a hidden secondary attack path.

findings.title: Hidden function with purpose 'runtime resolve' at 0x140008018
findings.description: Function at 0x140008018 performs 'runtime resolve' but is not reachable via the normal DriverEntry → MajorFunction call graph.  It may be invoked via a timer, DPC, work item, or kernel callback — a hidden secondary attack path.

findings.title: No PreviousMode / RequestorMode check detected
findings.description: Driver does not import ExGetPreviousMode or KeGetPreviousMode and no inline KTHREAD.PreviousMode read was found. Without checking PreviousMode, a kernel-mode caller could be impersonated, or user-mode requests may bypass validation. IOCTLs should verify RequestorMode == UserMode before trusting input.

findings.title: IOCTL handlers missing InputBufferLength validation
findings.description: The following IOCTL handlers do not appear to validate InputBufferLength before processing the buffer: 0x00322400, 0x00322404, 0x00322408, 0x0032240C. Without size checks, a short buffer could cause pool overread or out-of-bounds writes.

findings.title: IOCTL handlers missing NULL buffer check
findings.description: These IOCTL handlers may not check for NULL SystemBuffer before dereferencing: 0x00322400, 0x00322404, 0x00322408, 0x0032240C. A zero-length IOCTL request can result in SystemBuffer=NULL, causing a kernel NULL-pointer dereference (BSOD).

findings.title: No privilege check APIs imported
findings.description: Driver handles IOCTLs but does not import SeSinglePrivilegeCheck, SePrivilegeCheck, or SeAccessCheck. Sensitive operations should verify caller privileges beyond just device handle access rights.

findings.title: Driver has Authenticode signature
findings.description: PE contains a WIN_CERTIFICATE structure with embedded signature.

findings.title: Signed by: Insyde Software Corp.
findings.description:
Subject: Insyde Software Corp. (Insyde Software Corp.)
Issuer: Insyde Software Corp.
Serial: 2c4fe2755163848f4ad99fff4fb05390
Key: RSA 2048-bit
Valid until: 2024-09-01T11:36:09+00:00

findings.title: Signing certificate EXPIRED (valid until 2024-09-01T11:36:09+00:00)
findings.description: The code-signing certificate has expired. While drivers with a valid timestamp counter-signature remain valid after expiry, an expired cert without a timestamp means the signature is no longer trusted. This is also common in BYOVD — old vulnerable drivers with expired certs that were signed before revocation.

findings.title: Driver signed with SELF-SIGNED certificate
findings.description: The signing certificate is self-signed (issuer == subject). Self-signed drivers have no trust chain and cannot be verified by the OS certificate store. This requires test-signing mode to be enabled, which is a significant security downgrade. Malware often uses self-signed certs.

findings.title: PKCS#7 signature verifies cryptographically
findings.description: The primary Authenticode PKCS#7 signature was verified against the signer certificate's public key.

findings.title: Authenticode PE hash matches (binary not tampered)
findings.description: Recomputed sha256 hash of the PE image matches the value embedded in SpcIndirectDataContent.

findings.title: Nested signature(s) present: sha256, sha256, sha256, sha256, sha256, sha256
findings.description: The primary signature is dual-signed — a second (typically SHA-256) signature is nested via MsSpcNestedSignature. Windows on modern Windows 10+ / HVCI prefers the SHA-256 side for verification.

findings.title: Chain does not terminate at a Windows kernel-trusted root
findings.description: The certificate chain does not chain up to any root present in the Windows kernel trust store (20 known roots checked by thumbprint). Unless a missing intermediate can be supplied by the OS at load time, Windows will not accept this driver.

findings.title: Load restricted: Self-signed certificate
findings.description: This driver is self-signed. It will only load on Windows with test-signing mode enabled (bcdedit /set testsigning on). A watermark appears on the desktop when test-signing is active.

findings.title: HVCI incompatible: missing FORCE_INTEGRITY flag
findings.description: This driver is NOT compatible with Hypervisor-protected Code Integrity (HVCI / Memory Integrity). On systems with HVCI enabled, Windows will BLOCK this driver from loading. Issues: missing FORCE_INTEGRITY flag.

findings.title: Driver architecture: x64
findings.description: 64-bit driver — loads on x64 Windows

findings.title: Load compatibility: BLOCKED — 4 issue(s) prevent loading
findings.description:
[BLOCKED] Kernel Trust Anchor: Chain does not terminate at a Windows kernel trust root (no-ms-root)
  [BLOCKED] Self-Signed: Self-signed drivers require test-signing mode
  [BLOCKED] HVCI: Blocked when Memory Integrity is ON (missing FORCE_INTEGRITY flag)
  [BLOCKED] Secure Boot: Blocked when Secure Boot is enabled
  [PASS] Timestamp: Expired cert but timestamped by DigiCert Timestamp 2021

blockers: FORCE_INTEGRITY flag not set (HVCI requires it on the PE header)

passes: Any valid signature accepted under test-signing

blockers: No WHQL attestation EKU — S Mode / Secured-Core require WHQL-signed drivers
No Authenticode page hashes (Secured-Core requires per-page integrity)

findings.title: Device Access Security Audit

findings.title: ROP/JOP gadgets found: 611 total (misc: 185, reg-control: 158, memory-read: 127, memory-write: 101, stack-pivot: 30, jmp-reg: 10)
findings.description:
Found 611 usable ROP/JOP gadgets in executable sections. Since many drivers lack ASLR, these gadgets are at predictable addresses and can be chained for kernel exploitation.

Top gadgets by type:
  [stack-pivot] (30 found):
    0x140001124: pop rsp ; ret
    0x140001288: pop rsp ; pop rdi ; ret
    0x140001643: mov esp, ebx ; pop r15 ; pop r14 ; pop rdi ; ret
  [memory-write] (101 found):
    0x140001C8F: mov dword ptr [rcx + 0x18], eax ; ret
    0x140001C8E: mov qword ptr [r9 + 0x18], rax ; ret
    0x140001C8D: sub al, 0x49 ; mov dword ptr [rcx + 0x18], eax ; ret
  [memory-read] (127 found):
    0x14000111B: mov edi, dword ptr [rsp + 0x68] ; add rsp, 0x40 ; pop r12 ; ret
    0x14000111A: mov rdi, qword ptr [rsp + 0x68] ; add rsp, 0x40 ; pop r12 ; ret
    0x140001118: and al, 0x60 ; mov rdi, qword ptr [rsp + 0x68] ; add rsp, 0x40 ; pop r12 ; ret
  [reg-control] (158 found):
    0x140001123: pop r12 ; ret
    0x140001122: pop r12 ; ret
    0x140001120: add esp, 0x40 ; pop r12 ; ret
  [jmp-reg] (10 found):
    0x1400025FE: add byte ptr [rax], al ; jmp rax
    0x1400025FC: add byte ptr [rax], al ; add byte ptr [rax], al ; jmp rax
    0x1400025FA: test byte ptr [rax], al ; add byte ptr [rax], al ; add byte ptr [rax], al ; jmp rax
